provenance.composition_title: Nocturne in Eb Major, Op. 9 No. 2
provenance.verdict_reason: Nocturne in Eb Major, Op. 9 No. 2 (1832) by Frederic Chopin. Composition: US=public_domain, EU=public_domain. Arrangement by Bernd Krueger. License: CC-BY-SA. Evidence: https://piano-midi.de/. All initial public_candidate rules met. Awaiting Slice 2.5 verification: source URL resolves, license text preserved at source, license version confirmed. Until verified, treat as internal for distribution.

annotation_target.structure: Developmental consequent — descending resolution, extended trill
teaching_goals: pace the descent — don't rush the resolution

teaching_notes.note: Extended trill: relax the fingers, let it flow. Slow to match the ritardando.

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 21–24 of Nocturne in Eb Major, Op. 9 No. 2 (continuation of mm. 17–20), analyze the musical continuation, play in a loop.

session.content: Play measures 21–24 and describe how this middle section phrase resolves.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 21–24.
Measures 21–24 resolve the tension from mm. 17–20 with a long descent and the famous extended cadential trill. The mood settles as the melody falls to the midrange. Let me loop mm. 21–24.
Chopin Nocturne mm. 21–24: developmental consequent, descending resolution, extended trill. Pair with mm. 17–20.

eval_metadata.split_strategy: stratified by (composer, composition_id) with MIDI byte-hash dedup
